purpose: W4-E.3 Phase 2 mutation: cc_rules.json updates per Anish-signed audit-pass decisions on 2026-04-28.

decisions.item1_tamilNadu: ADD CCR-SW-TAMIL-NADU with sp-balu_r; Rajesh -> D-022
decisions.item2_hyderabad: DEFER -> D-022 (neither Kranthi nor Pooja Sharma mapped)
decisions.item4_ttt_all: DEFER -> D-022 (Shushankita not mapped); CCR-TTT-FEEDBACK stays narrow
decisions.item5_north_1_7: KEEP AS-IS per Anish (operational practice all-communications); D-021 SPOC-DB header fix
decisions.item6_grInternational: ADD CCR-NORTH-GR-INTERNATIONAL with sp-sahil; Pooja Sharma -> D-022; sp-sahil unconfirmed

added.sourceRuleText: Keep R. Balu and Rajesh in Cc for Tamil Nadu Schools
Keep Sahil Sharma, Pooja Sharma in Cc for GR International School

deferred.sourceText: Keep Kranthi and Pooja Sharma in Cc for Hyderabad Schools
deferred.reason: Neither "Kranthi" nor "Pooja Sharma" exists in sales_team.json or users.json. Anish: do not fabricate ccUserIds. D-022.

deferred.sourceText: Keep Shushankita in Cc for Schools in Maharashtra and all TTT Schools
deferred.reason: "Shushankita" not in sales_team.json or users.json. SW#5 is a compound rule; the Maharashtra sub-region portion needs Shushankita's ID before the rule can land. D-022.

deferred.sourceText: Keep Shushankita in Cc for Schools in all TTT Schools (East#2 + North#3 + SW#5 TTT portion)
deferred.reason: "Shushankita" not mapped. CCR-TTT-FEEDBACK stays narrow per Anish. CCR-TTT-ALL (training-mode TTT, all-communications) lands once Shushankita's ID is in users.json. D-022.

ccResolverUnionBehaviour.reference: src/lib/ccResolver.test.ts L330 "overlapping ccUserIds dedupe across multiple matching rules"
ccResolverUnionBehaviour.conclusion: Resolver iterates every matching rule, accumulates ccUserIds into a Set, then resolves Set -> emails (also a Set). Multiple matching rules produce union of users without duplication. CCR-TTT-FEEDBACK and CCR-TTT-ALL (when later added) combine cleanly.
